title: Security Alerts Ingestion Health from Vendor Feeds

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 SI-5 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 SI-5 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.14.37: Security Alerts Ingestion Health from Vendor Feeds.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.37' running on index=threat_intel OR sourcetype=vendor:security_advisory, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

index=threat_intel earliest=-7d
| stats latest(_time) as last_event, count by sourcetype, feed_name
| eval hours_since=round((now()-last_event)/3600,1)

description: Ensures security advisory and vendor alert feeds continue arriving for SI-5 security alerts, advisories, and directives.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes threat_intel and sourcetypes vendor:security_advisory`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Standardize `feed_name` at ingest; (2) alert per feed SLA; (3) document alternate notification path; (4) map to IR-4 playbooks when vendor declares incident. Indexes required: `index=threat_intel`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=vendor:security_advisory``.
visualization: Table (stale feeds), Time chart (events per feed), Single value (feeds in breach). Key fields: `feed_name`, `hours_since`, `last_event`, `sourcetype`.
knownFalsePositives: Security or system signals can spike during maintenance, vendor tests, and lab cutovers ‚Äî check scope, asset criticality, and the scan schedule before you call it a failure.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for TAXII (if used), vendor JSON via HEC, RSS parser custom.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=threat_intel` OR `sourcetype=vendor:security_advisory`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Standardize `feed_name` at ingest; (2) alert per feed SLA; (3) document alternate notification path; (4) map to IR-4 playbooks when vendor declares incident.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=threat_intel earliest=-7d
| stats latest(_time) as last_event, count by sourcetype, feed_name
| eval hours_since=round((now()-last_event)/3600,1)
| where hours_since>48
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Alerts Ingestion Health from Vendor Feeds (SI-5)** ‚Äî Ensures security advisory and vendor alert feeds continue arriving for SI-5 security alerts, advisories, and directives.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=threat_intel` OR `sourcetype=vendor:security_advisory`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for TAXII (if used), vendor JSON via HEC, RSS parser custom. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: threat_intel.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=threat_intel, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sourcetype, feed_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_since** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where hours_since>48` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (stale feeds), Time chart (events per feed), Single value (feeds in breach)
grandmaExplanation: We make sure security advisory and vendor alert feeds continue arriving for SI-5 security alerts, advisories, and directives. That helps federal and agency security reviews match what the systems actually do day to day.
